title: Materials Management (MM)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of the SAP MM module.
Identify important master data used in MM.
Learn commonly used MM transaction codes.

introduction.title: What is SAP MM?
introduction.content: SAP Materials Management (MM) is the module responsible for procurement, inventory management, material storage, and goods movement. It ensures that the right material is available at the right place and at the right time while maintaining optimal inventory levels.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP MM Used?
business_purpose.content: Every manufacturing organization depends on raw materials, consumables, and spare parts. SAP MM helps manage purchasing, inventory, vendors, and material movements efficiently while reducing shortages and excess stock.

master_data.name: Material Master
master_data.description: Stores complete information about a material.

master_data.name: Purchasing Info Record
master_data.description: Maintains purchasing conditions between vendor and material.

master_data.name: Source List
master_data.description: Specifies approved vendors for a material.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A production department requires stainless steel scrap. A Purchase Requisition is created, converted into a Purchase Order, materials are received through Goods Receipt, inspected by Quality Management, and then stored in inventory for production use.

best_practices: Verify vendor information before creating Purchase Orders.
Record Goods Receipt immediately after material arrival.
Monitor stock levels to avoid shortages.
common_mistakes: Posting Goods Receipt for the wrong material.
